CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.   
The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the reason for updating the document. Column 5 should include the names of the author(s) 
doing the changes. Including the name in column 6 shows that the document has been validated by the 
TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation means the 
document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
Sea Surface Temperature The temperature of the ocean water at 20 cm depth. 
Sea-Ice Surface 
Temperature 
The temperature of the sea-ice surface.  
Thermal skin layer The layer within which the long -wave radiation penetrates the ocean, 
typically less than 1mm, where ocean-atmosphere heat exchange takes 
place and temperature is measured using Infra-red sensors. 
SSTsub-skin Sea Surface Temperature at the base of the skin layer. 
SSTskin Sea Surface skin  temperature, which is the mean temperature of the 
thermal skin layer. 
SSTfoundation Sea Surface Temperature at the base of the thermal layer, the diurnal 
thermocline, which is considered representative of night -time and/or 
well mixed conditions, i.e free of any diurnal signal. 
Sea Ice Concentration  Fraction (percentage) of the grid cell where the sea surface temperature 
is quantified containing sea-ice.
